## Official skill

No official Microsoft skill exists for this topic. Dataverse plug-in authoring (IPlugin, Custom APIs, PAC CLI plugin workflow) is not covered by any official skill in the `dataverse:*` or `code-apps-preview:*` namespaces.

## Repository layout

```
<repo-root>/
└── plugins/
    └── <plugin-name>/           # One subfolder per Dataverse Plugin project
        ├── <PluginName>.csproj  # Scaffolded by pac plugin init
        ├── <PluginName>.snk     # Strong-name key
        ├── PluginBase.cs        # Generated base class
        └── Models/              # Early-bound classes (generated — never edit by hand)
```

- Each plugin project lives in its own subfolder. Never place `.csproj` files directly under `plugins/`.
- Scaffold every new project with `pac plugin init` — never create by hand.

## Domain scope

This plugin covers two distinct Dataverse extensibility mechanisms, both implemented as .NET assemblies:

| Skill | Covers |
|---|---|
| `ppbp-dv-plugin-build` | Project scaffolding, early-bound class generation, build workflow, deployment (PAC CLI) |
| `ppbp-dv-step-plugin` | Step plugin implementation: execution pipeline stages, pre/post images, service factory |
| `ppbp-dv-custom-api` | Custom API design: bound vs. unbound, request/response parameters, Power Automate callability |

## Global plug-in principles

1. **Always extend `PluginBase`** and override `ExecuteDataversePlugin` — never implement `IPlugin.Execute` directly in a `pac plugin init` project.
2. **Use early-bound classes** generated by `pac modelbuilder build` — never use late binding (`entity["attribute"]`). The generated `Models/` are build output: **never hand-edit them; always regenerate** when the schema changes.
3. **Never use `ColumnSet(true)`** in any Retrieve or image configuration — it bypasses column security and wastes bandwidth.
4. **Declare all working variables inside `ExecuteDataversePlugin()`** — Dataverse reuses plug-in instances; instance fields accumulate state across calls.
5. **Increment `AssemblyVersion` and `FileVersion` before every deployment** — without a version bump, Dataverse silently skips the update.

## Anti-patterns (DO NOT DO)

| Anti-pattern | Correct approach |
|---|---|
| Storing state in instance fields | Declare all variables inside `ExecuteDataversePlugin()` |
| Late binding (`entity["attribute"]`) | Use early-bound classes from `pac modelbuilder build` |
| Hand-editing a generated early-bound class in `Models/` — overwritten on the next regeneration and diverges from the live schema. | Never edit generated classes; re-run `pac modelbuilder build` to regenerate. |
| `ColumnSet(true)` anywhere | Always specify the exact columns needed |
| Creating plugin projects by hand | Always scaffold with `pac plugin init` |

## Skill boundaries

This skill covers plug-in domain orientation and repository layout. It does not cover:

- Project scaffolding, build workflow, deployment → `ppbp-dv-plugin-build`
- Step plugin execution pipeline, images, registration → `ppbp-dv-step-plugin`
- Custom API design and implementation → `ppbp-dv-custom-api`
- Dataverse schema design → `ppbp-dv-metadata-overview`
- Project repository layout → `ppbp-overview`
